summary.applicability_rationale: The web-conference application uses the WebRTC API (RTCPeerConnection, getUserMedia, MediaStream) with <video> elements for media capture, encoding, and rendering. It does not use the WebCodecs API (VideoEncoder, VideoDecoder, AudioEncoder, AudioDecoder, EncodedAudioChunk, EncodedVideoChunk, VideoFrame, AudioData, or ImageDecoder). All codec selection and media processing is handled internally by the browser's WebRTC implementation. Therefore, all normative sections of the W3C WebCodecs specification are not applicable to this codebase.

findings.requirement: Defines key terms used throughout the specification including codec, key frame, codec state, and other foundational terms for WebCodecs interfaces.

source_files.code_snippet:
// Room logic with WebRTC
class WebConference { ... }

evidence.observation: The application does not reference any WebCodecs terminology or interfaces. All media handling is done via WebRTC APIs (RTCPeerConnection, getUserMedia).
evidence.gap_description: No WebCodecs definitions are relevant because the application does not implement low-level codec access.

findings.remediation: No action required. These definitions would only become relevant if the application adopted the WebCodecs API.

findings.requirement: Defines the abstract codec processing model including control message ordering, codec work queues, and the relationship between configure, encode/decode, flush, and reset operations.

evidence.observation: The application acquires media using getUserMedia() and passes raw MediaStream objects directly to RTCPeerConnection via addTrack(). No explicit codec processing model is implemented because the browser's WebRTC stack handles all encoding/decoding internally.
evidence.gap_description: The codec processing model (configure, encode, decode, flush, reset lifecycle) is not implemented because WebRTC abstracts all codec operations.

findings.remediation: No action required. The codec processing model is handled transparently by the WebRTC implementation.

findings.requirement: Describes the background motivation for providing low-level codec access to web applications, separate from the higher-level abstractions of MediaStream and WebRTC.

evidence.observation: The application uses the higher-level MediaStream and WebRTC APIs that WebCodecs was designed to complement, not replace. The application has no need for frame-level codec access.
evidence.gap_description: No gap — the application appropriately uses the higher-level WebRTC abstraction for its video conferencing use case.

findings.requirement: Specifies the ordering of control messages (configure, encode/decode, flush, reset, close) and their effect on the codec state machine. Implementations MUST process control messages in order.

evidence.observation: No WebCodecs control messages (configure(), encode(), decode(), flush(), reset(), close()) are used anywhere in the codebase. Grep for 'VideoEncoder', 'VideoDecoder', 'AudioEncoder', 'AudioDecoder' yields zero results.
evidence.gap_description: The control message processing model is entirely absent because no WebCodecs interfaces are instantiated.

findings.requirement: Specifies that each codec instance has a parallel queue for offloading codec work to a separate thread, ensuring non-blocking operation on the main thread.

evidence.observation: No codec work queues are created because no WebCodecs codec instances exist in the application.
evidence.gap_description: Codec threading and parallel queue management is handled by the browser's WebRTC internals, not by application code.

findings.requirement: Defines the AudioDecoder interface for decoding encoded audio chunks into AudioData objects. Includes constructor, internal slots, attributes (state, decodeQueueSize), events (dequeue), and methods (configure, decode, flush, reset, close).

evidence.observation: The AudioDecoder interface is not used. Audio decoding of incoming WebRTC streams is performed automatically by the browser when remote audio tracks are added via ontrack event and played through <video> elements or Audio objects.
evidence.gap_description: No AudioDecoder instantiation or usage found in the codebase.

findings.remediation: No action required. AudioDecoder could be useful if custom audio processing (e.g., real-time transcription at the frame level) were needed.

findings.requirement: Defines the VideoDecoder interface for decoding encoded video chunks into VideoFrame objects. Includes constructor, internal slots, attributes (state, decodeQueueSize), events (dequeue), and methods (configure, decode, flush, reset, close).

evidence.observation: Video decoding is handled by assigning MediaStream objects to <video> element srcObject properties. The browser internally decodes the incoming WebRTC video frames. No VideoDecoder API is used.
evidence.gap_description: No VideoDecoder instantiation or usage found in the codebase.

findings.remediation: No action required. VideoDecoder would enable frame-level video manipulation (filters, compositing) but is not needed for basic conferencing.

findings.requirement: Defines the AudioEncoder interface for encoding AudioData objects into encoded audio chunks. Includes constructor, internal slots, attributes, events, methods (configure, encode, flush, reset, close), and EncodedAudioChunkMetadata.

evidence.observation: Audio encoding is performed by the WebRTC stack when local audio tracks from getUserMedia() are sent through RTCPeerConnection. No AudioEncoder API is used.
evidence.gap_description: No AudioEncoder instantiation or usage found in the codebase.

findings.remediation: No action required. AudioEncoder would be relevant for custom audio codec selection or pre-processing pipelines.

findings.requirement: Defines the VideoEncoder interface for encoding VideoFrame objects into encoded video chunks. Includes constructor, internal slots, attributes, events, methods (configure, encode, flush, reset, close), and EncodedVideoChunkMetadata.

evidence.observation: Video encoding is performed by the WebRTC stack internally when local video tracks are added to RTCPeerConnection via addTrack(). No VideoEncoder API is used.
evidence.gap_description: No VideoEncoder instantiation or usage found in the codebase.

findings.remediation: No action required. VideoEncoder could offer benefits like explicit codec control, SVC layer management, or custom bitrate adaptation, but these are handled automatically by WebRTC.

findings.requirement: Defines configuration dictionaries (AudioDecoderConfig, VideoDecoderConfig, AudioEncoderConfig, VideoEncoderConfig) and related types (CodecState, HardwareAcceleration, AlphaOption, LatencyMode, BitrateMode). Specifies how to check configuration support and clone configurations.

evidence.observation: No WebCodecs configuration objects are created. The application does not specify codecs, bitrate, latency mode, or hardware acceleration preferences because WebRTC handles all codec negotiation via SDP.
evidence.gap_description: All codec configuration is implicit via WebRTC SDP negotiation rather than explicit WebCodecs configuration objects.

findings.requirement: Provides the isConfigSupported() static method to query whether a given configuration is supported before instantiating a codec.

evidence.observation: No calls to isConfigSupported() exist. The application does not need to check codec support because WebRTC negotiation handles codec capability exchange.
evidence.gap_description: Configuration support checking is not applicable without WebCodecs usage.

findings.requirement: Describes how codec support information can be used for signaling codec capabilities between endpoints.

evidence.observation: Codec capability signaling is handled via SDP offer/answer exchange through the WebRTC signaling channel (Socket.io). WebCodecs configuration signaling is not applicable.
evidence.gap_description: No WebCodecs-based signaling mechanism is used.

findings.requirement: Defines the codec string format used to identify codecs in WebCodecs configurations. User agents MUST support the codecs described in the WebCodecs Codec Registry.

evidence.observation: No codec strings are specified anywhere in the application. WebRTC codec negotiation uses SDP m-lines and a]= attributes, not WebCodecs codec strings.
evidence.gap_description: Codec identification is handled by WebRTC SDP, not WebCodecs codec strings.

findings.requirement: Define the configuration dictionaries for audio and video encoders and decoders, including mandatory fields like codec string, sample rate, number of channels, coded dimensions, and optional fields like description, hardware acceleration, bitrate, and scalability mode.

evidence.observation: No encoder or decoder configuration dictionaries are constructed. All codec parameters are managed internally by WebRTC.
evidence.gap_description: None of the four configuration dictionary types are used in the codebase.

findings.requirement: Defines the HardwareAcceleration enum (no-preference, prefer-hardware, prefer-software) for controlling codec hardware acceleration preferences.

evidence.observation: Hardware acceleration is managed by the browser's WebRTC implementation. The application cannot and does not specify hardware acceleration preferences via WebCodecs.
evidence.gap_description: No hardware acceleration preference is set because WebCodecs is not used.

findings.remediation: No action required. If the application adopted WebCodecs, hardware acceleration preferences could optimize encoding performance.

findings.requirement: Defines the LatencyMode enum (quality, realtime) which controls whether the codec optimizes for throughput/quality or low-latency operation.

evidence.observation: The WebRTC stack internally optimizes for real-time latency. No explicit latency mode configuration is possible without WebCodecs.
evidence.gap_description: Latency optimization is handled implicitly by WebRTC, not through WebCodecs LatencyMode.

findings.requirement: Defines the CodecState enum (unconfigured, configured, closed) representing the lifecycle state of a codec instance.

evidence.observation: No codec instances with state tracking exist in the application.
evidence.gap_description: CodecState tracking is not applicable without WebCodecs codec instances.

findings.requirement: Defines EncodedAudioChunk and EncodedVideoChunk interfaces for representing compressed media data, including constructors, attributes (type, timestamp, duration, byteLength), and methods (copyTo).

evidence.observation: No EncodedAudioChunk or EncodedVideoChunk objects are created or processed. Encoded media is handled internally by WebRTC's RTP packetization layer.
evidence.gap_description: Encoded chunk handling is not applicable without WebCodecs usage.

findings.requirement: Defines raw media interfaces including the memory model for media resources.

evidence.observation: No raw media interface objects (AudioData, VideoFrame) are created or managed by the application.
evidence.gap_description: Raw media access is not used; the application works with MediaStream objects at a higher abstraction level.

findings.requirement: Specifies the memory model for raw media objects including reference counting, close() semantics, and transfer/clone behavior for AudioData and VideoFrame.

evidence.observation: No raw media objects requiring explicit memory management are created. MediaStream lifecycle is managed by the browser.
evidence.gap_description: Memory model requirements are not applicable without raw media object creation.

findings.requirement: Defines the AudioData interface for representing raw audio samples, including constructors, attributes (format, sampleRate, numberOfFrames, numberOfChannels, duration, timestamp), and methods (allocationSize, copyTo, clone, close).

evidence.observation: No AudioData objects are created. Audio is captured via getUserMedia() and passed directly to RTCPeerConnection.
evidence.gap_description: AudioData interface is not used.

findings.requirement: Defines the AudioSampleFormat enum (u8, s16, s32, f32, and planar variants) specifying supported raw audio sample formats.

evidence.observation: No audio sample format selection occurs in the application. Audio format handling is internal to the browser.
evidence.gap_description: Audio sample format specification is not applicable without WebCodecs AudioData usage.

findings.requirement: Defines the VideoFrame interface for representing raw video frames, including constructors (from ImageBitmap, BufferSource, etc.), attributes (format, codedWidth, codedHeight, displayWidth, displayHeight, timestamp, duration, colorSpace), and methods (metadata, allocationSize, copyTo, clone, close).

evidence.observation: No VideoFrame objects are created. Video frames are rendered by assigning MediaStream to <video> element srcObject. The application does not access individual video frames.
evidence.gap_description: VideoFrame interface is not used.

findings.remediation: No action required. VideoFrame could enable advanced features like background blur, virtual backgrounds, or frame-level effects.

findings.standard_section: 9.5-9.7. VideoFrame CopyTo Options / DOMRects / Plane Layout
findings.requirement: Defines options for copying video frame data including region selection, stride/offset specification via PlaneLayout, and DOMRect handling within VideoFrame.

evidence.observation: No video frame data copying operations are performed in the application.
evidence.gap_description: VideoFrame copy operations are not applicable without VideoFrame usage.

findings.requirement: Defines the VideoPixelFormat enum (I420, I420A, I422, I444, NV12, RGBA, RGBX, BGRA, BGRX) specifying supported raw video pixel formats.

evidence.observation: No pixel format selection or specification occurs in the application.
evidence.gap_description: Pixel format handling is internal to the browser's rendering pipeline.

findings.standard_section: 9.9-9.12. Video Color Space / Color Primaries / Transfer Characteristics / Matrix Coefficients
findings.requirement: Defines VideoColorSpace interface and related enums for describing color space properties of video frames including primaries (bt709, bt470bg, smpte170m, bt2020, smpte432), transfer characteristics (bt709, smpte170m, iec61966-2-1, linear, pq, hlg), and matrix coefficients.

evidence.observation: No color space management is performed. Color space handling is managed internally by the browser's video rendering pipeline.
evidence.gap_description: Color space configuration is not applicable without VideoFrame or WebCodecs usage.

findings.requirement: Defines the ImageDecoder interface for decoding images including animated formats. Includes ImageDecoderInit, ImageDecodeOptions, ImageDecodeResult, ImageTrackList, and ImageTrack interfaces.

evidence.observation: The application uses standard elements for image display (e.g., AI avatar SVG). No ImageDecoder API is used for image processing.
evidence.gap_description: ImageDecoder interface is not used; standard HTML image elements suffice.

findings.requirement: Specifies that user agents MAY reclaim resources held by WebCodecs objects (codecs, frames, chunks) under memory pressure, and the behavior requirements when this occurs.

evidence.observation: The application properly cleans up MediaStream tracks and peer connections on leave. No WebCodecs resources exist to reclaim.
evidence.gap_description: Resource reclamation for WebCodecs objects is not applicable.

findings.requirement: Addresses security considerations including the risk of codec vulnerabilities, side-channel attacks via timing information, and the need to validate untrusted encoded data.

evidence.observation: Since the application does not directly handle encoded media data, WebCodecs-specific security considerations (codec fuzzing, timing side channels on decode) do not apply. WebRTC security is handled by the browser.
evidence.gap_description: WebCodecs security considerations are not applicable. WebRTC provides its own security model (DTLS-SRTP).

findings.requirement: Addresses privacy considerations including fingerprinting risks from codec support enumeration and hardware capability detection.

evidence.observation: The application does not enumerate codec capabilities or expose hardware information via WebCodecs APIs, so the fingerprinting risks described in this section do not apply.
evidence.gap_description: Privacy considerations for WebCodecs codec enumeration do not apply.

recommendations.title: Consider WebCodecs for Advanced Video Processing
recommendations.description: If the application needs to add features like background blur, virtual backgrounds, or custom video filters, adopting the WebCodecs VideoEncoder/VideoDecoder APIs would provide frame-level access to video data. This would allow processing individual VideoFrames before encoding and sending via WebRTC's Insertable Streams (WebRTC Encoded Transform).

recommendations.title: Consider WebCodecs for Explicit Codec Control
recommendations.description: WebCodecs would allow the application to explicitly select codecs (e.g., AV1 for better compression, or hardware-accelerated H.264), configure bitrate and latency mode, and implement custom bitrate adaptation algorithms. This could improve video quality and reduce bandwidth usage compared to relying solely on WebRTC's automatic codec negotiation.

recommendations.title: Consider WebCodecs for Server-Side Recording
recommendations.description: If server-side recording or transcoding is needed in the future, WebCodecs could be used (in a worker context) to decode, process, and re-encode media streams. This would be an alternative to the current architecture where all media flows peer-to-peer.
